what_it_does: Renders a fixed SVG overlay that draws and updates a curved line linking a selected DOM node marked data-tether-point to its detail card marked data-tether-card, using a requestAnimationFrame loop that measures both elements in client coordinates and writes path attributes directly.
observations: Uses useEffect + useRef and starts a requestAnimationFrame loop whenever selectedSeq is present.
Queries document for [data-tether-point] and [data-tether-card] on every frame.
Computes the card anchor as the nearest point on the card rectangle to the dot center.
Offsets the line start by 7px from the dot center along the chord direction.
Bows the quadratic control point away from the .wheel-region center, falling back to a perpendicular bow.
Hides the tether when the dot-to-card distance is 18px or less.
Sets svg opacity and path/circle attributes imperatively; React does not re-render per frame.
Assumes a single point/card pair and a .wheel-region element exists for the hub-aware bow.
standouts: Design-critic intent to avoid radial-spoke illusion by bowing the arc outward from the wheel hub.
Renderer-agnostic contract based on data-tether-point and data-tether-card attributes.
Zero React re-renders per frame via rAF-driven DOM attribute writes.
conventions: Functional React component with TypeScript props.
DOM measurement and imperative updates confined to a useEffect.
Styling delegated to CSS classes (tether-overlay, tether-line, tether-node).
concerns: Global querySelector every frame is fragile with multiple tether elements and incurs layout cost.
Magic numbers (7, 18, 130, 0.22, 2.4) are not named constants.
rAF loop keeps running while selectedSeq is set even when elements are missing or the tether is hidden.
No ResizeObserver/IntersectionObserver; relies on continuous rAF for scroll and resize.
Pointer-events disabling is not visible in code and depends on external CSS.
notes: A React SVG overlay that uses a rAF loop to draw a hub-aware tether between a selected dot and its detail card without React per-frame renders.
questions: Is pointer-events: none enforced by the tether-overlay CSS class?
What assigns data-tether-point and data-tether-card, and can there ever be more than one pair?
Is .wheel-region guaranteed to exist in every lens that uses this component?
Why were the 7px start offset and 18px minimum length chosen?
Would a ResizeObserver or scroll listener be preferable to continuous getBoundingClientRect in rAF?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the stated purpose: it draws a presentation-only, pointer-events-off (by CSS) curve between a selected dot and its card, bows it away from the wheel hub, and updates via rAF to avoid React re-renders.
apparent_themes: Visual continuity between selection and detail
Performance by bypassing React's render loop
Design-critic attention to avoiding visual clutter/convergence
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the selected-point-to-card relationship immediately visible while keeping the line from disappearing into the wheel's radial geometry, and to do so cheaply across animations, scrolling, and resizing.
novelty: The radial-outward bow computed from the .wheel-region center so the tether arcs through open space rather than aligning with wheel spokes.
contribution: A reusable, renderer-agnostic SVG tether overlay that links any DOM element marked data-tether-point to one marked data-tether-card with a curved, context-aware line updated outside React's render cycle.
summary_for_embedding: Tether.tsx is a React component that renders a fixed SVG overlay and uses a requestAnimationFrame loop to draw a context-aware quadratic Bézier curve between a selected DOM node (data-tether-point) and its detail card (data-tether-card), bowing the arc away from a wheel hub.
intention_for_embedding: To visually anchor a selected point to its off-wheel detail card while keeping animation, scroll, and resize updates performant and renderer-independent.

symbols.does: Renders the SVG tether overlay and manages its requestAnimationFrame update lifecycle based on selectedSeq.
symbols.description: React component that holds refs to the SVG, path, and anchor circle, starts a rAF loop when a selection exists, and hides the overlay when selectedSeq is absent.

symbols.does: Computes and writes the tether geometry each animation frame.
symbols.description: Measures the selected point and card via getBoundingClientRect, finds the nearest card anchor, offsets the start to the dot's edge, picks a bow direction away from the .wheel-region hub (or a perpendicular fallback), builds a quadratic Bézier path, positions the anchor ring, and toggles SVG opacity based on link length.
